name: Manawyddan Extended
description: Manawyddan fab Llŷr is a prominent figure in Welsh mythology, appearing primarily in the Second Branch of the Mabinogi, *Branwen ferch Llŷr*. He is the brother of Branwen and Bran the Blessed, and a member of the Island of the Mighty. After the devastating war in Ireland, which claims the lives of Bran and most of his retinue, Manawyddan returns to Wales with the surviving seven, including Pryderi, Cigfa, and Taliesin. He marries Pryderi's mother, Rhiannon, becoming a step-father to Pryderi. Manawyddan is known for his resilience, adaptability, and skill in crafts. He demonstrates his ability to survive and provide for himself and his companions through various trades, including saddle-making, shield-making, and shoemaking, though his success is often undermined by misfortune and the machinations of Llwyd ap Cil Coed, who seeks revenge for Pryderi's imprisonment. Manawyddan's patience and resourcefulness are ultimately tested when the land is repeatedly blighted by magical attacks, forcing him to use his cunning to capture a magical mouse responsible for the devastation, leading to a confrontation and resolution of the feud with Llwyd. His story highlights themes of loss, perseverance, and the importance of justice and reconciliation. He embodies the qualities of a skilled craftsman, a loyal companion, and a man who can overcome adversity through wit and determination.
subtitle: Resilient craftsman and brother of Branwen
achievements: Provided for his companions through craft
Negotiated peace with Llwyd ap Cil Coed

keyMyths.title: Return from Ireland
keyMyths.description: Manawyddan, along with seven survivors, returns to Wales after the devastating war in Ireland, facing the challenge of rebuilding their lives in the aftermath of immense loss.

keyMyths.title: The Blighted Lands
keyMyths.description: The land of Dyfed is repeatedly blighted by magical attacks, testing Manawyddan's resourcefulness as he seeks to provide for himself and his companions amidst the hardship.

keyMyths.title: The Capture of the Mice
keyMyths.description: Manawyddan captures the magical mice responsible for the blight, leading to a confrontation with Llwyd ap Cil Coed and the resolution of their feud.

sources.author: Gwyn Jones and Thomas Jones
sources.description: A translation of the Mabinogion, a collection of early Welsh prose stories.
sources.text: A translation of the Mabinogion, a collection of early Welsh prose stories.

sources.author: John Matthews
sources.work: Taliesin: Shamanism and the Bardic Mysteries in Britain and Ireland
sources.description: Explores the role of Taliesin and his connection to other figures in Welsh mythology.
